title: Victor Serge Was One of the Great Revolutionary Writers
summary: Many readers will be familiar with Victor Serge’s literary work: his novels, notably The Case of Comrade Tulayev, and his fascinating autobiography Memoirs of a Revolutionary. All his work centers around the great historical events of the first half of the twentieth century, the hopes aroused by the Russian Revolution of 1917, and its subsequent […]
reason: A literary retrospective on a historical revolutionary writer is primarily representational content — new framing of an old figure — with no concrete routing-level event or structural mechanism to analyze.

title: Dockworkers Against Russia’s and Israel’s Wars
summary: Ahead of May Day 2022, two months after Russia’s full-scale attack on Ukraine, Ukrainian trade unionists called for solidarity with the invaded country. Artem Tidva, a Ukrainian left-winger and labor activist, addressed international unions calling for actions to help stop the Russian war machine. In Sweden, such action was already underway. In March 2022, Swedish […]
reason: Dockworkers refusing to handle war materiel is a concrete routing-level intervention — redirecting the flows of logistics and supply chains that sustain military violence — not merely symbolic messaging.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: dockworkers aren't making statements, they're physically interrupting supply chains, which is structurally distinct from representational solidarity and shows the commune layer operating through labor's positional power in global logistics.

title: Babies Born from Dead Parents Will Increase with New Tech. Are We Ready?
summary: Reproductive technologies have enabled children to be posthumously conceived from the frozen eggs and sperm of deceased parents, raising legal, ethical, and practical questions.
